candidate.foreshadow_text: “It is this,” I answered, producing a trowel from beneath the folds of my _roquelaire_.
candidate.payoff_text: As I said these words I busied myself among the pile of bones of which I have before spoken. Throwing them aside, I soon uncovered a quantity of building stone and mortar. With these materials and with the aid of my trowel, I began vigorously to wall up the entrance of the niche.
candidate.foreshadow_summary: Montresor produces a trowel from under his cloak as a sign of being a mason, which Fortunato dismisses as a jest.
candidate.payoff_summary: Montresor uses the same trowel to wall up the entrance of the niche, entombing Fortunato alive.

proposed_trigger.description: Montresor starts using the trowel for masonry
observable_conditions: P0074: 'With these materials and with the aid of my trowel, I began vigorously to wall up the entrance of the niche.'

candidate.relation_description: The trowel, initially presented as a joke, becomes the instrument of murder.
candidate.stage1_rationale: The trowel appears in a seemingly playful context and is later repurposed with deadly intent, spanning a significant narrative gap.

final_trigger.description: Montresor begins using the trowel for masonry
observable_conditions: P0074: 'With these materials and with the aid of my trowel, I began vigorously to wall up the entrance of the niche.'

verdict.foreshadow_description: In P0065, Montresor produces a trowel, seemingly as a jest about being a mason, but its significance is not yet revealed.
verdict.payoff_description: In P0075, Montresor uses the trowel to wall up Fortunato in the niche, revealing the trowel's true purpose as a murder weapon.
verdict.rationale: The trowel is introduced as an unexplained detail, then recontextualized when used to entomb Fortunato. The trigger is the moment Montresor begins masonry work with the trowel, observable in the narrative. The setup and payoff are separated by several paragraphs and a change in location, preventing immediate causality. Returning to the earlier moment, the trowel's presentation is clearly a foreshadowing of the method of murder. The connection is not merely thematic or a character reappearance; the trowel transitions from a prop in a jest to an instrument of death.

candidate.foreshadow_text: The thousand injuries of Fortunato I had borne as I best could; but when he ventured upon insult, I vowed revenge. You, who so well know the nature of my soul, will not suppose, however, that I gave utterance to a threat. _At length_ I would be avenged; this was a point definitively settled—but the very definitiveness with which it was resolved, precluded the idea of risk. I must not only punish, but punish with impunity. A wrong is unredressed when retribution overtakes its redresser. It is equally unredressed when the avenger fails to make himself felt as such to him who has done the wrong.
candidate.payoff_text:
“Yes,” I said, “let us be gone.”

“_For the love of God, Montressor!_”

“Yes,” I said, “for the love of God!”
candidate.foreshadow_summary: The narrator declares that revenge must be accomplished without risk to himself and the victim must know who is avenging him.
candidate.payoff_summary: Fortunato, realizing his fate, cries out 'For the love of God, Montresor!' acknowledging Montresor as his murderer, while Montresor goes unpunished.

observable_conditions: P0082: 'For the love of God, Montresor!'

candidate.relation_description: The specific rules of revenge set at the very beginning are fulfilled when the victim identifies his killer at the climax.
candidate.stage1_rationale: The opening philosophical statement directly maps to the climactic recognition, demonstrating delayed fulfillment across the entire narrative.

final_trigger.description: Fortunato calls out Montresor's name in a desperate plea
observable_conditions: P0083: 'For the love of God, Montresor!'

verdict.foreshadow_description: The narrator vows revenge and explicitly states that the avenger must make himself felt as such to the wrongdoer, or the wrong remains unredressed.
verdict.payoff_description: At the climax, Fortunato, realizing he is being entombed alive, cries out Montresor's name, thereby satisfying the condition of the avenger making himself known.
verdict.rationale: P0001 establishes a specific narrative rule for revenge, unresolved at the time. P0083 provides new narrative fulfillment when Fortunato's plea confirms he recognizes Montresor as his punisher, with a non-trivial temporal gap and clear structural connection beyond thematic echo.

candidate.foreshadow_text:
I said to him: “My dear Fortunato, you are luckily met. How remarkably well you are looking to-day! But I have received a pipe of what passes for Amontillado, and I have my doubts.”

“How?” said he. “Amontillado? A pipe? Impossible! And in the middle of the carnival!”

“I have my doubts,” I replied; “and I was silly enough to pay the full Amontillado price without consulting you in the matter. You were not to be found, and I was fearful of losing a bargain.”

“Amontillado!”

“I have my doubts.”

“Amontillado!”

“And I must satisfy them.”

“Amontillado!”

“As you are engaged, I am on my way to Luchesi. If any one has a critical turn, it is he. He will tell me—”
candidate.payoff_text:
“The Amontillado!” ejaculated my friend, not yet recovered from his astonishment.

“True,” I replied; “the Amontillado.”
candidate.foreshadow_summary: Montresor lures Fortunato with the promise of a pipe of Amontillado, exploiting his wine expertise.
candidate.payoff_summary: After chaining Fortunato, Montresor ironically echoes 'The Amontillado' and begins walling him in, revealing the lie; there is no wine.

proposed_trigger.description: Montresor stops pretending about the Amontillado and starts masonry
observable_conditions: P0074: 'As I said these words I busied myself among the pile of bones... I soon uncovered a quantity of building stone and mortar.'

candidate.relation_description: The false promise of wine leads to a fatal trap, and the payoff is the unmasking of the lure.
candidate.stage1_rationale: The central deception is set up early and paid off when the truth is revealed through action rather than explanation, creating dramatic irony.

final_trigger.description: Montresor stops pretending about the Amontillado and starts masonry
observable_conditions: P0075: 'As I said these words I busied myself among the pile of bones... I soon uncovered a quantity of building stone and mortar.'

verdict.foreshadow_description: Montresor lures Fortunato with the false promise of Amontillado.
verdict.payoff_description: Fortunato, fettered, calls out about the Amontillado, and Montresor replies 'True' before beginning to wall him in, revealing the lure was a trap.
verdict.rationale: The false Amontillado bait is introduced in the foreshadow and left unresolved. At the payoff, Montresor's action of walling Fortunato in immediately after acknowledging the Amontillado confirms the bait was a deadly deception, resolving the earlier promise. The temporal and causal distance, along with the trigger of Montresor dropping the pretense and starting masonry, satisfies all criteria for a true narrative payoff.
